sources.title: Valkey Official Documentation
keyInsights: Comprehensive reference for all Valkey commands and data types
Covers persistence, replication, cluster, security, and more
Source of truth for configuration directives and defaults

sources.title: Valkey Data Types Documentation
keyInsights: Strings, hashes, lists, sets, sorted sets, streams, geospatial, bitmaps, HyperLogLog, bloom filters
Extension via Lua functions and modules
Each type has specific encoding optimizations for small sizes

sources.title: Valkey Commands Reference
keyInsights: Full taxonomy: bitmap, bloom filter, hash, list, set, sorted set, stream, string, HyperLogLog, JSON, geospatial, cluster, connection, server, transactions, pub/sub, scripting, search, generic
Each command page includes complexity, syntax, examples, and version history

sources.title: Valkey Memory Optimization
keyInsights: Compact encodings save up to 10x memory for small aggregates
Hash-based storage with bucket splitting reduces per-key overhead dramatically
32-bit instances halve pointer sizes but limit to 4GB
Always set maxmemory and maxmemory-policy

sources.title: Valkey Key Eviction Documentation
keyInsights: 8 eviction policies: noeviction, allkeys-lru, allkeys-lfu, volatile-lru, volatile-lfu, allkeys-random, volatile-random, volatile-ttl
Approximated LRU with configurable sampling (maxmemory-samples)
LFU uses Morris counters with tunable lfu-log-factor and lfu-decay-time
allkeys-lru is best general-purpose policy

sources.title: Valkey Persistence Documentation
keyInsights: RDB: compact snapshots, fast restarts, potential minute-level data loss
AOF: append-only log with everysec/always/no fsync policies
Hybrid: RDB preamble in AOF for best of both
Production recommendation: both RDB + AOF for PostgreSQL-level safety

sources.title: Valkey Pipelining Documentation
keyInsights: Up to 10x throughput improvement over sequential commands
Reduces RTT overhead and system call context switching
Batch size recommendation: ~10k commands per batch
Memory trade-off: server queues responses

sources.title: Valkey Transactions Documentation
keyInsights: MULTI/EXEC provides atomic command batching
WATCH enables optimistic locking (CAS pattern)
No rollback support - by design for simplicity and performance
Pre-EXEC errors abort transaction; post-EXEC errors don't stop other commands

sources.title: Valkey Lua Scripting Documentation
keyInsights: Embedded Lua 5.1 interpreter with atomic execution
EVAL for ad-hoc scripts, EVALSHA for cached scripts
server.call() vs server.pcall() for error handling
All accessed keys must be declared in KEYS array for cluster compatibility
Script flags via Shebang for read-only and stale-data control

sources.title: Valkey Functions Documentation
keyInsights: Functions are first-class database elements that persist and replicate
FUNCTION LOAD registers libraries; FCALL invokes functions
Libraries are atomic units - updated as a whole with REPLACE flag
Preferred over EVAL for production: no per-call compilation, survives restarts

sources.title: Valkey Distributed Locks (Redlock) Documentation
keyInsights: Redlock algorithm: acquire lock on N/2+1 independent instances
SET resource NX PX 30000 with random value
Valkey 9.0: DELIFEQ replaces Lua script for safe lock release
Retry with random delay to prevent thundering herd
Clock drift and fencing token considerations

sources.title: Valkey Streams Introduction
keyInsights: Append-only log with O(1) insertion and O(log N) range queries
Consumer groups for load-balanced, acknowledged message processing
Time-based IDs (milliseconds-sequence) enable efficient time-range queries
500K-1M insertions/sec with pipelining; 99.9% latency <= 2ms
MAXLEN and MINID trimming strategies

sources.title: Valkey Pub/Sub Documentation
keyInsights: At-most-once delivery - messages lost if no subscriber listening
Pattern subscriptions via PSUBSCRIBE for wildcard matching
Sharded pub/sub (SSUBSCRIBE/SPUBLISH) for cluster-friendly scoped messaging
Regular pub/sub broadcasts to all cluster nodes; sharded stays within shard

sources.title: Valkey Client-Side Caching Documentation
keyInsights: Server-assisted tracking with invalidation messages
Default mode: per-client key tracking (uses server memory)
Broadcast mode: prefix-based subscriptions (zero server memory)
RESP3 push messages or separate Pub/Sub connection for invalidation
NOLOOP option prevents self-invalidation; OPTIN for selective caching

sources.title: Valkey Migration from Redis Guide
keyInsights: Compatible with Redis OSS 2.x through 7.2.x
Three strategies: physical (RDB copy), replication (REPLICAOF), selective (MIGRATE)
Redis 7.4+ incompatible - requires RIOT or RedisShake
Cluster migration: add Valkey replicas, CLUSTER FAILOVER, remove Redis nodes

sources.title: Valkey 8.0 GA Announcement
keyInsights: No backward-incompatible changes to command syntax or responses
Triple throughput to 1M+ RPS via I/O threading overhaul
First major release developed entirely in open source
lazyfree-lazy-user-del defaults to yes

sources.title: Valkey 8.1 GA Announcement
keyInsights: New hashtable: 20-30 bytes less per key-value pair, ~10% throughput improvement
SET IFEQ conditional update saves roundtrip for compare-and-set
TLS negotiation offloaded to I/O threads: 300% faster connection acceptance
COMMANDLOG extends SLOWLOG with large request/reply tracking
ZRANK 45% faster, BITCOUNT 514% faster (AVX2), PFMERGE 12x faster (AVX)

sources.title: Valkey 9.0 Feature Introduction
keyInsights: Atomic slot migration: entire slots move atomically via AOF format
Numbered databases in cluster mode
Hash field expiration with HEXPIRE, HSETEX, HGETEX families
DELIFEQ for conditional delete
Up to 1 billion RPS across 2000 nodes
40% throughput gain from pipeline memory prefetch

sources.title: Valkey Hash Field Expiration Deep Dive
keyInsights: Per-field TTL with 16-29 bytes overhead per expiring field
Coarse bucket design with adaptive encoding
Active background expiration only - no lazy expiration on access
No performance regression on standard HGET/HSET operations
Compatible with Redis 8.0 hash field expiration API

sources.title: Valkey Numbered Databases in Cluster Mode
keyInsights: Key names belong to a specific database but slot assignment is key-based only
Zero overhead when unused
No resource isolation - noisy neighbor problem persists
MOVE command is O(1) - useful for atomic key replacement
Limited ACL and metrics support currently

sources.title: Valkey I/O Threading Performance Analysis
keyInsights: 230% throughput increase (360K to 1.19M RPS) with 8 I/O threads
69.8% latency decrease (1.792ms to 0.542ms)
Thread affinity improves memory locality
Command execution remains single-threaded

sources.title: Valkey Performance Optimization Methodology
keyInsights: Eliminate redundant code, reduce lock contention, address false sharing
Asynchronous processing and batch operations
CPU-specific SIMD vectorization for hot paths
Network is often the bottleneck before CPU

sources.title: Valkey Security Documentation
keyInsights: Protected mode rejects non-localhost connections without password
ACL system for fine-grained user permissions
TLS support on all channels including cluster bus
Never expose Valkey directly to untrusted networks

sources.title: Valkey ACL Documentation
keyInsights: Per-user command and key pattern restrictions
Default user has full access with no password
TLS client certificate authentication maps to ACL users
Pub/Sub channel access control via &pattern

sources.title: Valkey Diagnosing Latency Issues
keyInsights: SLOWLOG for command execution time exceeding threshold
LATENCY LATEST and LATENCY DOCTOR for event monitoring
Fork stalls, I/O blocks, and memory pressure as latency causes
Dynamic threshold adjustment for debugging

sources.title: Valkey Keyspace Documentation
keyInsights: Keys are binary-safe strings up to 512MB
Colon-delimited namespacing convention (user:1000:profile)
Very long keys (>1KB) are a bad idea for performance
Hash tags {tag} for cluster slot co-location

sources.title: Valkey Client Libraries
keyInsights: GLIDE: official multi-language client (Rust core + Java/Python/Node.js/Go bindings)
Single multiplexed connection per node with auto-pipelining
Existing Redis clients (ioredis, Jedis, redis-py, go-redis) fully compatible
GLIDE 2.0 adds Go, OpenTelemetry, and batching support

sources.title: Valkey Bloom Filters Module
keyInsights: Rust-based module compatible with Valkey 8.0+
Scaling and non-scaling bloom filter modes
BF.ADD, BF.EXISTS, BF.RESERVE commands
Use cases: deduplication, fraud detection, username checks

sources.title: Valkey JSON Module Documentation
keyInsights: Native JSON document storage with JSONPath queries
JSON.SET, JSON.GET, JSON.MSET, JSON.MGET, JSON.DEL
Most operations O(1) with direct paths
Enhanced JSONPath syntax recommended for new development

sources.title: How Valkey 8.1 Handles 50 Million Sorted Set Inserts
keyInsights: Sorted sets use dual data structure: skip list + hash table
O(log N) for all mutations
ZADD with SIMD fast_float optimization in 8.1

sources.title: Valkey vs Redis: How to Choose in 2026
keyInsights: Valkey enhanced I/O multithreading for multi-core utilization
Redis 8 integrates query engine, vector search, time series natively
Valkey 8.1 Swiss Tables hashtable for 20% memory efficiency
Choose Valkey for open source, performance, community governance

sources.title: Percona: Valkey/Redis Not-So-Good Practices
keyInsights: Never use KEYS in production - use SCAN instead
Avoid HGETALL and SMEMBERS on large collections - use HSCAN/SSCAN
Hot keys in cluster cause imbalanced load
Disable FLUSHDB/FLUSHALL in production configs
Always set maxmemory to prevent OOM kills

sources.title: Percona: Rate Limiting Strategies with Valkey/Redis
keyInsights: Fixed window: INCR + EXPIRE, simplest approach
Sliding window counter: two keys with weighted overlap
Token bucket: Lua function for burst tolerance
Leaky bucket: strict constant-rate output
Fall back to local in-memory limits when Valkey unavailable

sources.title: Percona: Sorted Sets - Leaderboards and Beyond
keyInsights: Sorted sets for leaderboards, time-ordered feeds, priority queues
Score can represent timestamp, priority, price, or distance
O(log N) insertion/retrieval - fast even at millions of elements
Dual skip list + hash table implementation

sources.title: Percona: Accelerating Applications with Valkey Caching
keyInsights: Cache-aside pattern for database query caching
Session storage with hash + TTL
Short-lived data benefits from automatic expiration

sources.title: Percona: Practical Guide to Valkey Configuration and Production Tuning
keyInsights: Default install is just the beginning - production requires deliberate config
Memory, networking, persistence, security must all be tuned
Common pitfalls for experienced DB administrators

sources.title: AWS: Valkey GLIDE 2.0 Announcement
keyInsights: Go language support added
OpenTelemetry integration for observability
Pipeline batching reduces network overhead
Single multiplexed connection per node

sources.title: Google Cloud: Best Practices for Memorystore for Valkey
keyInsights: Batch with pipelining instead of one-by-one commands
Use connection pools to reuse connections
Network throughput often the bottleneck before CPU
Set affinity between NIC queues and CPU cores

sources.title: Momento: Designing Smarter Caches with Valkey 9.0 Numbered Databases
keyInsights: Numbered databases as namespaces - same key in different databases never collides
Database number is part of connection state, not key name - saves memory vs prefixes
No resource isolation, no per-database metrics, limited ACL
Per-database rate limiting enables multi-tenant isolation

sources.title: Valkey 2025 Year-End Review
keyInsights: 346 active contributors in 2025
Valkey 9.0 launched with 1 billion+ RPS clusters
2026 focus: easier operations, multi-threading, deeper OSS connections
